title: [InstCombine] Consolidate another fold into select value equivalence
summary: The developers generalized a select-icmp optimization by extracting it into a dedicated helper (`foldSelectICmpEq`) that handles both scalar and vector cases uniformly. The key insight was recognizing that the pattern `(X == C) ? X : Y -> (X == C) ? C : Y` applies not just to scalars but also to vectors—including those with `undef` elements—as long as the transformation is safe. Safety required ensuring that when `OldOp` (the compared value) is constant, we don't introduce undefined behavior by propagating `undef` incorrectly. The patch removed the original ad-hoc folding in `foldSelectInstWithICmp` and delegated to the generalized helper, which already contained logic to handle `undef` via `simplifyWithOpReplaced`. This approach centralizes the logic, avoids duplication, and cleanly extends to vectors by leveraging existing constant-folding utilities that respect `undef` semantics.
